lines: Python  ·  XGBoost  ·  Supabase  ·  GitHub Actions  ·  Streamlit  ·  TradingView API
This project is a fully autonomous stock market intelligence system built to identify equities with high probability of
single-day explosive gains (20%+). It combines real-time data collection, machine learning prediction, and continuous
self-improvement into a zero-touch daily pipeline orchestrated entirely via GitHub Actions.
Each trading day the system screens up to 1,500 stocks through TradingView, computes 97 technical indicators via
yfinance intraday bars, scores every candidate with an XGBoost classifier, and stores ranked predictions before
pre-market opens. After market close it records outcomes, tracks accuracy, and every Sunday retrains the model on all
accumulated data — including a novel mistake-learning module that over-weights past false positives and false
Data collection, model training, GitHub Actions
CI/CD, and Supabase persistence. The brain of the
operation — runs headlessly every trading day.

Every trading day the system collects labeled training examples across two classes and stores them in 9 Supabase
tables. Winners are the top-15 daily gainers; non-winners are a deliberately diverse sample of flat, slight-gain, slight-loss,
Top-15 gainers: symbol, OHLCV, change %, detection timestamp
Negative examples: 30% flat, 30% slight gain, 20% slight loss, 20% big loss

The ml_mistake_learner module fetches the model's own past prediction errors from Supabase and injects them into weekly
retraining with 3x (false positive) and 2x (false negative) sample weights — targeting the model's specific blind spots rather than
Training uses the most recent 15% of data as validation, enforcing temporal separation. This fixed a critical issue where random
splits allowed the model to 'see' same-week stocks in both train and val, producing deceptive 0.9999 AUC scores.
A single t1_column_map.py file bridges two naming schemas: the ta-library short names stored in Supabase (e.g. 'rsi', 'stoch.k')
and the model's expected long names (e.g. 'RSI_14', 'STOCHk_14_3_3'). Duplicate alias resolution prevents column conflicts
After each market day, ml_track_comprehensive_accuracy derives optimal screening thresholds (min HV, min volume ratio, RSI
range) from the last 90 days of winner stats. These are committed to the repo as learned_filters.json and applied next morning's
screen run, creating a closed feedback loop.
A stock that moved +58% intraday but closed outside top-N was previously mislabelled as a false positive. The
INTRADAY_WIN_THRESHOLD=15% rule upgrades these rows to label=1 with 1.5x sample weight, correcting the model's
